source_paper_title: Optimal Budget Aggregation with Star-Shaped Preference Domains
context:
Section 5.2 (\(\ell_\infty\) preferences), page 15 (PDF page 15 / 44 in the provided parsed text)

We conjecture that the incompatibility of efficiency, strategyproofness, and weak fairness conditions holds for \(\ell_p\) disutilities for any \(1 \le p \le \infty\), when \(m \ge 3\) and \(n \ge 3\).
title: Impossibility of efficiency, strategyproofness, and proportionality for \(\ell_p\) disutilities with \(m\ge 3\) and \(n\ge 3\)
latex:
Given integers \(n\ge 3\) and \(m\ge 3\), let \(N=\{1,\dots,n\}\) be a set of agents and \(M=\{1,\dots,m\}\) a set of alternatives. Let \(\Delta^m:=\{q\in\mathbb{R}^m_{\ge 0}: \sum_{j\in M} q_j = 1\}\) be the \(m\)-simplex of feasible budget distributions.

Each agent \(i\in N\) has a peak (ideal distribution) \(p_i\in\Delta^m\). For a fixed \(p\in[1,\infty]\), define the \(\ell_p\) disutility of agent \(i\) for an outcome \(q\in\Delta^m\) by
\[
 d_i(q) := \|p_i-q\|_p,
\]
where for \(p\in[1,\infty)\), \(\|x\|_p := \left(\sum_{j\in M} |x_j|^p\right)^{1/p}\), and for \(p=\infty\), \(\|x\|_\infty := \max_{j\in M} |x_j|\). Agent \(i\)'s preference ranks outcomes by smaller disutility.

A (tops-only) budget aggregation mechanism is a function \(f:(\Delta^m)^n\to \Delta^m\) that maps every profile \(P=(p_1,\dots,p_n)\) to an outcome \(q=f(P)\).

Define:
- Strategyproofness: for every profile \(P\), every agent \(i\in N\), and every misreport \(p_i'\in\Delta^m\), letting \(P'=(p_i',p_{-i})\), it holds that \(\|p_i-f(P)\|_p \le \|p_i-f(P')\|_p\).
- Pareto efficiency: for every profile \(P\), there is no \(q'\in\Delta^m\) such that \(\|p_i-q'\|_p \le \|p_i-f(P)\|_p\) for all \(i\in N\) and \(\|p_{i^*}-q'\|_p < \|p_{i^*}-f(P)\|_p\) for some \(i^*\in N\).
- Proportionality: for every single-minded profile \(P\) (i.e., every \(p_{i,j}\in\{0,1\}\)), the outcome satisfies \(f(P)_j = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{i\in N} p_{i,j}\) for all \(j\in M\).

problem (conjecture): Prove or disprove that for every \(p\in[1,\infty]\), there exists no mechanism \(f\) that simultaneously satisfies Pareto efficiency, strategyproofness (w.r.t. \(\ell_p\) disutilities), and proportionality whenever \(m\ge 3\) and \(n\ge 3\).
location_in_paper: Section 5.2 (\(\ell_\infty\) preferences), page 15 (PDF page 15 / 44 in the provided parsed text)
